response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned software engineer with nearly two decades of broad experience, but his profile is a poor match for this role's core requirement: deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; his career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial protocol experience, all of which are central to the position.
strengths: Strong systems background with low-latency, high-throughput services (DLang, async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and mentoring/teaching experience
Experience with event-driven architectures and distributed platforms (GISCollective, microservices)
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++ experience (brief role ending in 2015); C++ not even listed in skills
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure; no MQTT or industrial protocol experience
Career trajectory is web/full-stack focused, diverging from embedded/industrial automation domain

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and mentoring track record
